title: Capturing Structural Locality in Non-parametric Language Models

structured.summary: The paper proposes a method to improve non-parametric kNN language models by learning linear transformations of retrieval distances based on hierarchical structural locality features. Evaluated on Wikipedia text and Java source code, the approach yields consistent perplexity and token prediction accuracy improvements by biasing retrieval towards more locally related neighbors.

strengths.point: Incorporation of structural locality into retrieval improves language modeling.
strengths.evidence: Experiments show 3.9% relative perplexity gain on Wikipedia and 2.3% on Java code when using learned locality-based distance modifications.

strengths.point: Simple and lightweight method with few additional parameters.
strengths.evidence: Only a small set of linear parameters per locality level are learned without retraining the underlying parametric LM.
Method is validated on both natural language (Wikipedia) and programming language (Java) datasets with domain-specific locality features.

strengths.point: Detailed analysis of locality’s impact on retrieval quality.
strengths.evidence: Authors show that locality features better separate relevant neighbors and improve top-k retrieval accuracy.

weaknesses.point: Lack of comparison to prior metric learning approaches for retrieval.
weaknesses.evidence: No discussion or experiments comparing to learned distance metrics or attention reweighting methods in kNN-LMs.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient justification for using linear distance transformations.
weaknesses.evidence: The choice of a simple linear function is not motivated or compared against more expressive nonlinear functions.
Evaluation focuses on perplexity and token accuracy on only two datasets; lacks retrieval-specific metrics and broader domain tests.

weaknesses.point: Sparse details on reproducibility and experimental setup.
weaknesses.evidence: Optimization hyperparameters and datastore construction/indexing details are not fully specified.

weaknesses.point: Dependence on availability and quality of structural locality metadata.
weaknesses.evidence: Method requires explicit hierarchical locality features which may not exist or be reliable in many real-world datasets.

weaknesses.point: No analysis of robustness to domain shifts or scalability to large/dynamic datastores.
weaknesses.evidence: Potential overfitting to locality features and challenges with evolving locality structures are not addressed.

weaknesses.point: Clarity issues in defining and extracting locality features.
weaknesses.evidence: Descriptions of locality encoding and extraction processes are vague and assume domain knowledge.
Improvements are presented without confidence intervals or significance tests.

questions.question: How does the proposed linear distance modification compare to existing metric learning approaches for retrieval in kNN-LMs?
questions.motivation: Understanding relative benefits and limitations is important for positioning the contribution and potential improvements.

questions.question: Can the method generalize to datasets lacking clear hierarchical locality or with overlapping/non-hierarchical locality structures?
questions.motivation: Assessing applicability beyond structured domains is critical for broader impact.

questions.question: How robust is the approach to noisy or incomplete locality metadata, and what strategies can mitigate such issues?
questions.motivation: Real-world datasets often have imperfect metadata, affecting method reliability.

questions.question: Would more expressive (e.g., nonlinear or context-dependent) distance transformations yield further gains?
questions.motivation: Exploring richer parameterizations could improve retrieval quality beyond linear adjustments.

questions.question: How does the approach scale with very large or dynamically changing datastores?
questions.motivation: Practical deployment requires handling datastore growth and evolving locality structures efficiently.

critique_points.point_002: Insufficient justification for using linear distance transformations.. The choice of a simple linear function is not motivated or compared against more expressive nonlinear functions

critique_points.point_006: No analysis of robustness to domain shifts or scalability to large/dynamic datastores.. Potential overfitting to locality features and challenges with evolving locality structures are not addressed

critique_points.point_008: No statistical significance testing reported.. Improvements are presented without confidence intervals or significance tests
